title: Using ggerror
text: Base ggplot2 gives several different error geoms, using means deciding orientation, axis error follow, wire lower upper bounds correctly. creates overhead cluncky DX. ggerror collapses one layer one error-focused API. Everything vignette uses mtcars, can copy examples directly.

title: Using ggerror
text: geom_error() infers orientation data. one axis discrete numeric, error expands along numeric axis. default geom errorbbar, triggers hood geom_errorbar().
code: set.seed(1) mt <- mtcars[sample(nrow(mtcars), 5), ] mt$rn <- rownames(mt)  base_theme <- theme_minimal() + theme(   plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, family = "consolas", size = 12),   axis.title = element_blank() ) p <- ggplot(mt, aes(mpg, rn)) +   geom_point() + base_theme  p + geom_error(aes(error = drat))

title: Using ggerror
text: can pick base range geom either error_geom via one pinned wrapper functions.   Supported values error_geom "errorbar", "linerange", "crossbar", "pointrange". 💡 Tip: Make use geom_error() functional programming approach using error_geom argument purrr::map().
code: p + geom_error(aes(error = drat), error_geom = "crossbar") p + geom_error_crossbar(aes(error = drat)) supported_geoms <- c('errorbar', 'crossbar', 'linerange', 'pointrange') purrr::map(supported_geoms, \(err) p + geom_error(aes(error = drat), error_geom = err)) ## [[1]] ##  ## [[2]] ##  ## [[3]] ##  ## [[4]]

title: Using ggerror
text: Use error_neg error_pos negative positive extents differ.  error_neg extends negative direction error_pos extends positive direction, regardless whether error horizontal vertical. must supplied together. deal orientation min/max reduces overhead ensures stay focused displaying data, rather mechanics plot.
code: p + geom_error(aes(     error_neg = drat / 2,     error_pos = drat)     )

title: Using ggerror
text: Set unused side NA genuine one-sided error bar — ggerror auto-suppresses cap stem side.  Passing 0 instead NA still works soft-deprecated since v1.0.0 — ’ll get migration warning, ’d also need width_neg = 0 hide shared cap. NA cleaner idiom.
code: p +   geom_error(aes(     error_neg = NA,     error_pos = drat   ))

title: Using ggerror
text: finer control, negative positive sides can styled separately fixed _neg _pos parameters colour, fill, linewidth, linetype, alpha, width.
code: p +   geom_error(aes(     error_neg = drat / 2,     error_pos = drat),      colour_neg = "steelblue",     colour_pos = "firebrick",     linewidth = 2 # you can still control both sides of the error bar   ) p +   geom_error(aes(     error_neg = drat / 2,     error_pos = drat),          error_geom = "crossbar",     fill_neg = "grey90",     fill_pos = "grey30",     width_neg = 0.2,     width_pos = 0.6   )

title: Using ggerror
text: know , need, new error geom, please open issue GitHub repository. first motiviation simplify heck error geoms, reducing aesthetics single error aesthetic. rest (asymmetric, one-sided, etc) just niceties added along way.

title: Residual plots with stat_error() and sign_aware
text: Pass raw observations let stat compute error. default mean_se — mean one standard error:  Swap mean_ci 95% normal-theory confidence interval (Hmisc dependency):  can also pass custom function following ggplot2’s fun.data contract — takes numeric vector returns single-row data frame columns y, ymin, ymax:  stat_error() geom_error(stat = "error") equivalent — use whichever reads better context.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   stat_error() ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   stat_error(fun = "mean_ci", error_geom = "pointrange") iqr <- function(y) {   data.frame(     y    = median(y),     ymin = stats::quantile(y, 0.25, names = FALSE),     ymax = stats::quantile(y, 0.75, names = FALSE)   ) }  ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   stat_error(fun = iqr, error_geom = "crossbar")

title: Residual plots with stat_error() and sign_aware
text: residual plot, center x/y fitted value let bar extend toward observed value. Positive residuals route error_pos, negative error_neg, opposite side auto-suppressed:  bar points fitted observed: sign encodes direction, length encodes magnitude. axes numeric, pass orientation = "x" get vertical bars (error extends along y, observed axis). Colour halves separately make direction even obvious:
code: model <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(fitted(model), mpg)) +   geom_point() +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2, colour = "grey50") +   geom_error(aes(error = resid(model)),              sign_aware = TRUE, orientation = "x") +   labs(x = "Fitted", y = "Observed") ggplot(mtcars, aes(fitted(model), mpg)) +   geom_point() +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2, colour = "grey50") +   geom_error(     aes(error = resid(model)),     sign_aware = TRUE,     orientation = "x",     colour_neg = "firebrick",     colour_pos = "steelblue"   ) +   labs(x = "Fitted", y = "Observed")

title: Residual plots with stat_error() and sign_aware
text: sign_aware routes signed per-row values, stat_error() summarises raw observations bounds — sign route. Combining raises classed error (ggerror_error_sign_aware_with_stat).

code: @Manual{,   title = {ggerror: Extended Error Geoms for 'ggplot2'},   author = {Yann Cohen},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://github.com/iamYannC/ggerror}, }

title: Extended Error Geoms for ggplot2
text: ggerror simplifies ggplot2’s error geoms introduces asymetric error bars customization. Instead wiring ymin / ymax xmin / xmax hand, supply error (error_neg + error_pos) ggerror rest . can simple providing single error argument, yet offer full customization options per-side styling.

title: Extended Error Geoms for ggplot2
code: pak::pak('iamyannc/ggerror')  library(ggplot2) library(ggerror)  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point()  # Symmetric error bars, using default geom errorbar p + geom_error(aes(error = drat))  # Asymmetric error bars, using geom_error_pointrange and per-side styling p + geom_error_pointrange(aes(error_neg = drat / 2, error_pos = drat, linetype_neg = "dashed"))  # One-sided error bars, using the error_geom argument p + geom_error(error_geom = "linerange", aes(error_neg = NA, error_pos = drat))

title: Extended Error Geoms for ggplot2
text: detailed examples symmetric, asymmetric, one-sided, per-side styling, see vignette("ggerror").

title: Extended Error Geoms for ggplot2
text: package developed assistance AI tools. code reviewed author, remains responsible quality. Ideas new geoms welcome.

what: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
text: thin wrapper around ggplot2::geom_errorbar(), ggplot2::geom_linerange(), ggplot2::geom_crossbar(), ggplot2::geom_pointrange() accepts single error aesthetic figures orientation data. asymmetric errors, use error_neg + error_pos instead error.

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
code: geom_error(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   stat = "identity",   position = "identity",   ...,   error_geom = "errorbar",   orientation = NA,   sign_aware = FALSE,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )  geom_error_linerange(..., error_geom)  geom_error_crossbar(..., error_geom)  geom_error_pointrange(..., error_geom)

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created ggplot2::aes(). data data displayed layer. stat statistical transformation use data. Defaults "identity". position Position adjustment. ... arguments passed ggplot2::layer(). error_geom One "errorbar" (default), "linerange", "crossbar", "pointrange". Chooses ggplot2 error geom geom_error() dispatches hood. orientation Either NA (default; inferred data), "x" (vertical error), "y" (horizontal error). sign_aware TRUE, signed values error routed per row: positive values extend bar positive direction, negative values extend negative direction, opposite side suppressed. Useful residual plots x/y fitted value bar extends toward observed value. Incompatible stat = "error". Default FALSE. na.rm FALSE, missing values removed warning. show.legend Logical. layer included legends? inherit.aes FALSE, overrides default aesthetics.

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
text: Session-level knobs 0 -> NA migration. Set via options(): ggerror.silent_zero_warning — TRUE suppresses soft deprecation fired error_neg error_pos set 0 (encouraged set NA). Default FALSE. ggerror.zero_threshold — Numeric absolute tolerance zero-value detection. Values magnitude threshold treated exactly zero, triggering warning. Defaults 1e-8.

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
text: geom_error() requires x, y, one : error — symmetric half-width applied along non-categorical axis. error_neg error_pos — asymmetric; bar extends error_neg negative direction error_pos positive direction along non-categorical axis. one-sided bar, set unused side NA — cap, stem, shared-bound cap side suppressed. Mixing error error_neg / error_pos error, providing one asymmetric pair. Fixed per-side styling can supplied ... _neg _pos suffixes colour, fill, linewidth, linetype, alpha, width. fixed scalar parameters, mapped aesthetics.

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error = drat))   ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error = drat), error_geom = "pointrange")   # Asymmetric: bar extends drat/2 below and drat above each point ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error_neg = drat / 2, error_pos = drat))   # One-sided: set the unused side to NA (cap + stem auto-suppressed) ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error_neg = NA, error_pos = drat))   # Summarise raw data: mean +/- SE per group (see also stat_error()) ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   geom_error(stat = "error", error_geom = "pointrange")   # Signed residual plot: bar extends from fitted toward observed model <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars) ggplot(mtcars, aes(fitted(model), mpg)) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error = resid(model)),              sign_aware = TRUE, orientation = "x")   # Style the negative and positive halves separately ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(     aes(error_neg = drat / 2, error_pos = drat),     colour_neg = "steelblue",     colour_pos = "firebrick"   )

what: ggerror: Extended Error Geoms for 'ggplot2' — ggerror-package
title: ggerror: Extended Error Geoms for 'ggplot2' — ggerror-package
text: Extends 'ggplot2' error geoms. geom_error() accepts 'error' aesthetic auto-inference orientation. also supports 'error_neg' 'error_pos' asymmetric cases, full control aesthetics per side, color, width etc... package also includes vignette covering main usecases - symmetric, asymmetric, one-sided, per-side styling.

title: ggerror: Extended Error Geoms for 'ggplot2' — ggerror-package
text: Maintainer: Yann Cohen yannco5@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: Summarising stat for geom_error() — stat_error
text: stat_error() computes error bounds raw observation-level data using ggplot2's fun.data contract. geom_error() expects pre- computed error columns, stat_error() summarises y (x, orientation horizontal) within group via function supplied fun.

title: Summarising stat for geom_error() — stat_error
code: stat_error(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   geom = NULL,   position = "identity",   ...,   fun = "mean_se",   fun.args = list(),   error_geom = "errorbar",   orientation = NA,   na.rm = FALSE,   conf.int = 0.95,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )

title: Summarising stat for geom_error() — stat_error
text: mapping, data, position, show.legend, inherit.aes Standard ggplot2 layer arguments. geom geom render summary . Defaults GeomErrorStat, reuses geom_error()'s draw path. ... Additional parameters. Names match fun's formals (name, fun accepts ...) forwarded fun; remainder go geom_error() per-side styling (colour_neg, width_pos, …) standard aesthetics. fun One "mean_se" (default, uses ggplot2::mean_se()), "mean_ci" (mean 95% normal-theory CI via stats::qt(); Hmisc dependency), function taking numeric vector returning single-row data.frame columns y, ymin, ymax. fun.args Named list extra arguments pass fun. Merged ... arguments whose names match fun's formals; fun.args wins collision. error_geom One "errorbar" (default), "linerange", "crossbar", "pointrange". orientation NA (default, inferred), "x", "y". na.rm TRUE, drop NA values summarised axis applying fun. conf.int Confidence level forwarded fun function accepts conf.int argument (e.g. fun = "mean_ci" custom fun.data formal). Ignored funs declare , safe leave default using fun = "mean_se".

title: Summarising stat for geom_error() — stat_error
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) + stat_error() #> `stat_error()` using fun = "mean_se".   ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   stat_error(fun = "mean_ci", error_geom = "pointrange") #> `stat_error()` using fun = "mean_ci" and conf.int = 0.95.   # 90% CI with NA-tolerant summarising: ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   stat_error(fun = "mean_ci", conf.int = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ggerror 1.0.0
text: New stat_error() summarises raw observation-level data error bounds. Accepts fun = "mean_se" (default), "mean_ci" (95% normal- theory CI, Hmisc dep), custom function following ggplot2’s fun.data contract. Also available via geom_error(stat = "error"). New sign_aware = TRUE routes signed per-row values (typically residuals) one-sided bars whose direction encodes sign. Enables one-layer lm() residual plots. aes(error_neg = NA, error_pos = ...) now canonical idiom one-sided bars — cap stem NA side auto-suppress. Passing 0 still renders emits soft deprecation warning; silence options(ggerror.silent_zero_warning = TRUE) tune detection threshold via options(ggerror.zero_threshold = ...). New diagnostics: NA values symmetric error now warn row indices (class ggerror_warn_error_na); negative values without sign_aware abort row indices migration suggestion (class ggerror_error_negative_error_aes). New vignette lm-residuals covering stat_error() sign_aware lm() residual-plot demo.

title: ggerror 0.4.0
text: Added pkgdown site https://iamyannc.github.io/ggerror/.

title: ggerror 0.3.0
text: ggerror now includes complete aestetic support one sided error bars. Added fixed per-side styling parameters error bars: colour_neg / colour_pos, fill_neg / fill_pos, linewidth_neg / linewidth_pos, linetype_neg / linetype_pos, alpha_neg / alpha_pos, width_neg / width_pos. Fixed bug 0.2.0: supplying one-sided error bars, unused side’s width can set 0, default, example geom_error(aes(error_neg = 0, error_pos = se), width_neg = 0). Added vignette("ggerror") covering symmetric, asymmetric, one-sided, per-side styling workflows.

title: ggerror 0.2.0
text: New error_neg / error_pos aesthetics asymmetric error bars. bar extends error_neg negative direction error_pos positive direction along non-categorical axis. One-sided bars supported setting unused side 0 explicitly (e.g. aes(error_neg = 0, error_pos = se) upward-bar). existing symmetric error aesthetic unchanged. Combining error either error_neg / error_pos, providing one asymmetric pair, raises error.

title: ggerror 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Provides geom_error() (plus geom_error_linerange(), geom_error_crossbar(), geom_error_pointrange()) wrapping ggplot2’s error geoms behind single error aesthetic automatic orientation inference.
